channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Two Indians arrested by ICE: One for attempted child abuse, another for $60,000 theft
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Sponsors of Scottish football club jailed after being revealed as leaders of £12m cocaine trafficking ring
item.description: Two Lanarkshire businessmen who publicly projected success through sponsorships and luxury branding were unmasked as organisers of an £11 million cocaine trafficking network after EncroChat messages exposed their operation. Despite maintaining a respectable corporate image, greed and excess drove their criminal enterprise. Their prison sentences reinforce a warning: organised crime eventually collapses under scrutiny, regardless of wealth, image or influence.
item.dc:creator: TOI Sports Desk

item.title: The 8,000-year-old underground cave in Southern Europe holds a mystery: People entered and never returned
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists explore whether quantum physics could allow messages to travel back in time
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: UK travel boss jailed over £300k holiday 'ponzi scheme' used to pay children’s school fees
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: China sentences former defence ministers to death in sweeping military graft purge
item.description: China has handed down death sentences with a two-year reprieve to former defence ministers Wei Fenghe and Li Shangfu for corruption. This significant development is part of President Xi Jinping's extensive anti-graft campaign targeting the military and political elite. Both officials were found guilty of graft-related offenses, with Li Shangfu also accused of seeking improper benefits in personnel appointments.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Middle East tensions: Iran’s response to US peace proposal expected today
item.description: Iran is reviewing a US proposal, with a response expected soon, potentially via Pakistani mediators. Tehran prioritizes a full war cessation and sanctions relief over nuclear talks, seeking UN Security Council guarantees.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Come in person': How a bank gave Pope Leo a reality check
item.description: Leo identified himself as Robert Prevost. The bank employee asked the required security questions, listened to him, and then delivered the verdict familiar to anyone who has ever dealt with institutional procedure: he would have to come in personally.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship; 40 passengers disembark on South Atlantic island
item.description: Dutch officials confirmed about 40 passengers disembarked on St. Helena after a deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship, including the wife of a deceased Dutch man who later died in South Africa. Another passenger who disembarked at St. Helena tested positive for the virus in Switzerland.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Hantavirus cruise outbreak: Two Britons self-isolate after exposure scare on MV Hondius
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Ex-UK police officer accused of scamming Dior, Cartier and Apple in refund fraud
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Life 2 meters underground: Scientists discover hidden microbial oasis beneath Chile’s driest desert
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: This ancient Chinese megacity hides mysterious underground tunnels buried for over 4,000 years
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'They found nothing': Jeffrey Epstein's alleged suicide note released
item.description: A federal judge has ordered the release of a handwritten note, purportedly from Jeffrey Epstein, found nearly five years after his death. The note, discovered by his former cellmate, contains the line, "It is a treat to be able to choose ones time to say goodbye." The document was unsealed after a request by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Tyler “sym” Porter cause of death: Twitch streamer and TSM Valorant pro killed during birthday trip
item.description: Tyler Porter, a rising Valorant professional and Twitch streamer known as “sym,” tragically died in a car accident on May 5 while celebrating his 21st birthday. The former TSM player was widely respected for his viral Jett gameplay and competitive success. Fellow player Austen also reportedly died in the crash. Tributes from teammates, friends, and the esports community quickly flooded social media, remembering sym as a talented player and beloved friend with a bright future ahead.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: “Pretty easy to clear up”: Candace Owens questions Blake Neff over Erika Kirk TPUSA leadership video after Charlie Kirk death
item.description: Candace Owens and Blake Neff publicly clashed on X over Charlie Kirk’s alleged succession video and TPUSA leadership controversy. Owens demanded proof that Charlie Kirk approved Erika Kirk as successor, while Neff accused Owens of spreading conspiracy-driven attacks against the organization after Kirk’s death.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: What pushed Australia to reportedly cancel Sneako’s visa? streamer responds with bold clarification
item.description: Streamer Nico Sneako became part of a major controversy after reports claimed Australia cancelled his visa and issued a lifetime ban. Australian minister Tony Burke reportedly took the action over concerns related to hateful content. Sneako denied being deported and said he voluntarily left Australia after finishing his South East Asia trip and meetings in Sydney and Melbourne.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: Who is Jane Fonda? Ted Turner’s ex-wife who once called him her 'favorite ex-husband'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Clavicular charged in shocking Everglades shooting stream, faces up to 1 year in jail
item.description: Kick streamer Braden Eric Peters, also known as Clavicular, is in legal trouble. He is charged with unlawfully firing a gun in a public place. The incident occurred in the Florida Everglades during a livestream. Wildlife officials investigated the viral video. Peters' lawyers state he followed a guide's instructions and no harm occurred. The case highlights livestream consequences.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: 'Comparing H-1B workers...': Row over claim visa holders are less than 0.5% of US jobs
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
